title: luma bracteosa
intro: Luma bracteosa, also known as Chilean myrtle, is a small evergreen shrub native to the Andes mountains in Chile and Argentina. It is valued for its medicinal properties, which include its antimicrobial, anti-inflammatory, and antiseptic properties. The health benefits of Luma bracteosa include its ability to help reduce stress and anxiety, improve respiratory issues such as bronchitis, and support digestive health. Herbal preparations of Luma bracteosa include teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be used to make cough syrups, salves, and topical creams for skin issues.
